page: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highcpu-48 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the n4d family. It features 48 dedicated virtual CPUs (vCPUs) paired with 96.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core, aligning with compute-optimized hardware profiles designed for CPU-bound tasks.

This server configuration does not include local NVMe SSD storage or integrated GPU hardware accelerators. Additionally, it does not bundle complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Network and storage operations rely entirely on external block storage and network-attached resources.

Given the dedicated CPU allocation and the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the n4d-highcpu-48 is structured for workloads requiring high computational density without massive memory footprints. Suitable applications include batch processing, distributed compute jobs, web serving, and CI/CD pipelines. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs makes it a cost-efficient option for pure CPU processing tasks where local high-speed disk I/O or hardware acceleration is unnecessary.

This hardware profile is designed for high-density CPU tasks, making it suitable for workloads such as batch processing, web serving, and CI/CD pipelines.
